mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0% over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid quarterly increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, which reduces expected corporate profits and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and credit availability, which boosts near-term corporate revenue expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.04 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which triggers positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during tightening cycles, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 52% in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high consumer price index levels combined with elevated policy rates create a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and raises discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered cases, or the interaction effect shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls signals expanding economic activity and rising consumer spending capacity, which directly supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no correlation with 20-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.02) across multiple business cycles, or positive payroll changes coincide with negative equity returns > 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP growth rate falling below 1.0 percent indicates severe demand contraction and recessionary momentum, which triggers immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth below 1.0% fails to predict negative 5-day returns, or the threshold signal produces a positive mean return in > 50% of triggered instances during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in industrial production and retail sales confirms synchronized supply-side recovery and demand absorption, which reduces inventory buildup risks and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint indicator fails to predict positive returns when both series rise, or the interaction term out-of-sample Rank IC remains below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.
